world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 8
major_event: The Seer departs Stonebreak for the Twin Cities — a journey of two or three days through the passes. He carries only his staff and the phrase he was given to speak. The world does not pause for his arrival.

primary_event.name: The Seer Takes the Road
primary_event.summary: The Seer departs Stonebreak on foot, moving toward Caeris Thorne in the Twin Cities. He carries a single message: death walks the land. He will not arrive for two days. The world moves without him.

supporting_events.name: The Entity Calls Louder
supporting_events.summary: The hammering below Lostfeld has increased in frequency and resonance. Three more garrison soldiers deserted at dawn. Ironmaul's furious correspondence with the Druids continues; he has not slept in two days.

supporting_events.name: Velorn's Unauthorized Envoy Reaches the Ridge
supporting_events.summary: The Velorn clan envoy has reached the Frostvale ridge and is attempting to speak with the captive Arkenor lieutenant without Lythara's sanction. Elowen's scouts have spotted him and are deciding whether to detain or allow contact.

supporting_events.name: Rowen's Key and the Sinking Timeline
supporting_events.summary: Rowen's fleet has not moved from the deep anchorage. He has opened his chest twice but not removed the key. His navigator reports the island's waterline has dropped another hand-width overnight.

active_events.name: Deep Entity: Calling Phase
active_events.summary: The hammering is now a summons. Kessa bleeds in her sleep. Ironmaul's Druid correspondence accelerates. The gate breathes warmth into the mountain snow.
active_events.consequences: Each tick without response risks deeper infiltration of Kessa's mind. Druid communication may reveal the shared language connection, changing everything.

active_events.name: The Seer Takes the Road
active_events.summary: The Seer walks toward Caeris Thorne carrying the phrase 'death walks the land.' He will not arrive for 2 more ticks. His absence from Stonebreak is noticed.
active_events.consequences: His delay means Caeris remains without outside counsel while the court collapses. The Gnome Syndicate may intercept or shadow him on the road.

active_events.name: Twin Cities Succession Paralysis
active_events.summary: Roderic remains sealed in the chamber with Aldric. Minor lords correspond with Tidefall. Caeris stands vigil. Court confidence has collapsed.
active_events.consequences: Every tick of paralysis empowers Tidefall and Aldric's faction. A decisive royal act — death, decree, or emergence — could rebalance or shatter the succession.

active_events.name: Grand Druid Reads the Name
active_events.summary: The Grand Druid reads nightly from the pre-Accord book in the Entity's language. Comprehension grows. He has not yet realized he is reading the Entity's own name.
active_events.consequences: When comprehension reaches critical threshold, the Conclave may inadvertently speak the name aloud in ritual — with unknown consequences for the gate at Lostfeld.

active_events.name: Tidefall's Quiet Advance
active_events.summary: The Admiral receives minor lords' correspondence with growing satisfaction. A revised harbor treaty is being drafted. Dur Khadur envoys remain aboard the flagship.
active_events.consequences: If the treaty is presented before Roderic dies or acts, it creates a legitimate-seeming instrument of annexation that Caeris cannot easily void.

active_events.name: Velorn's Unauthorized Ridge Envoy
active_events.summary: Velorn dispatched an envoy to the Frostvale ridge to interrogate the Arkenor lieutenant without Lythara's knowledge. Elowen's scouts have detected him.
active_events.consequences: If Elowen detains the envoy, Velorn's resentment escalates toward open defiance of Lythara. If she allows contact, the Arkenor's testimony may fracture the Shadow Court.

active_events.name: Rowen's Key and the Accelerating Descent
active_events.summary: Rowen holds a literal key from the sinking island. The island descends measurably faster. He has not acted yet but is watching and planning.
active_events.consequences: Whatever the key unlocks may be below the waterline. Tidefall's sea lanes pass near the island; disruption there affects the Admiral's supply chains.

active_events.name: Gnome Syndicate Intelligence Assessment
active_events.summary: The Syndicate knows Caeris's dreams through a servant informant. They are now calculating who pays most for this knowledge — Tidefall, Caeris himself, or another party.
active_events.consequences: Selling to Tidefall accelerates the harbor-burning narrative's political weaponization. Selling to Caeris gives the Prince leverage but confirms the Syndicate as spies in his household.

faction_actions.action: Departs Stonebreak on foot along the mountain road toward the Twin Cities, carrying the message 'death walks the land' for Caeris Thorne.
faction_actions.reason: God-directive received. Must physically travel; cannot deliver at distance.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul dispatches a sealed secondary missive to the Monastery of Druids, describing the exact cadence and rhythm of the hammering in precise notation, asking if the Druids recognize the pattern.
faction_actions.reason: The Druid response gave him enough hope to push further. He suspects they know more than they said.

faction_actions.action: Grand Druid convenes an emergency inner Conclave session to re-examine the pre-Accord book's language against Ironmaul's original missive — searching for structural correspondence.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul's detailed description of the hammering triggered a recognition the Grand Druid cannot yet name. He must verify before replying.

faction_actions.action: Elowen orders her ridge scouts to detain the Velorn envoy quietly — no violence, no formal arrest, simply 'escort him to a tent and give him food and no answers' until she decides what to do.
faction_actions.reason: She cannot allow unauthorized Shadow Court contact with the Arkenor lieutenant before she understands what the lieutenant told her fully.

faction_actions.action: Lythara summons Velorn and Nythariel clan heads to a private court session upon discovering the envoy was dispatched without her knowledge — she intends to reassert clan discipline before it becomes defiance.
faction_actions.reason: Velorn's unauthorized action is a direct challenge to her authority. She must move before it becomes precedent.

faction_actions.action: Caeris Thorne, receiving no word from inside the chamber, writes a formal proclamation of regency on his own authority and seals it — but does not publish it yet, holding it as a contingency.
faction_actions.reason: He cannot act while his father lives, but the court is dissolving. He needs a prepared instrument.

faction_actions.action: The Admiral sends a courier to two of the most senior minor lords who contacted him, inviting them to a private meeting aboard a neutral merchant vessel offshore — bypassing the royal court entirely.
faction_actions.reason: Minor lord correspondence creates a lever; a face-to-face meeting converts that into a coalition. The Admiral moves to formalize his position before Roderic acts.

faction_actions.action: Rowen orders his navigator to chart the island's submerged eastern approach and map any structure beneath the waterline, using small diving boats in the predawn hours.
faction_actions.reason: The key implies a lock. The lock is likely submerged. He needs to know where before he uses the key.

faction_actions.action: The Syndicate dispatches a shadow-agent to trail the Seer on the mountain road — not to interfere, but to observe what he carries and what he knows, and report back.
faction_actions.reason: The Seer's sudden departure from Stonebreak immediately after Caeris's dreams became known suggests the two events are connected. The Syndicate trades in connections.

faction_actions.action: Dur Khadur envoys aboard Tidefall's flagship quietly offer the Admiral a 'consultancy' — experienced mercenary advisors for the Twin Cities transition, framed as professional service, not intervention.
faction_actions.reason: Aldric's alliance means Dur Khadur stands to profit from a Tidefall-aligned succession. They want a formal stake before the treaty is signed.

faction_actions.action: Farrock's garrison commander quietly doubles the watch on the mountain approaches from Lostfeld's direction, citing 'unusual vibration reports from the northern trade scouts.'
faction_actions.reason: Word of the hammering's change has reached Farrock through trader gossip. They do not understand it but they recognize threat-adjacent patterns.

faction_actions.action: A Vilefin scouting band slips east from the Rock Plains toward the Lostfeld foothills, drawn by the warmth radiating from the direction of the gate — they interpret the heat as a forge, a forge means metal, metal means trade or theft.
faction_actions.reason: Practical opportunity-seeking consistent with Vilefin behavior. They do not know what the warmth is.

faction_actions.action: A Vampire embedded in the Twin Cities court approaches one of the minor lords who wrote to Tidefall and offers 'discreet counsel' on how to position oneself for the succession — for a private consideration.
faction_actions.reason: Succession chaos is peak harvesting season for influence. The Vampires never act overtly but always position themselves at the hinge-point of power transitions.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities minor lords / succession positioning

faction_actions.action: Elowen sends a private rider to request a Druid emissary, specifically asking for one who has studied deep-earth resonance phenomena — she frames it as academic, not emergency.
faction_actions.reason: She watched Lostfeld's direction from the ridge and now wants expert interpretation of what the warmth and the gate mean for Frostvale's position.

recent_events.text: The Seer departed Stonebreak at first light, walking north along the mountain road with his staff and no escort. He carries one message. He will not arrive for two days.
Ironmaul has not slept. He sends a second missive to the Druids — this time with the hammering's rhythm written out in precise musical notation. His hands are shaking. Kessa has stopped eating.

Elowen's scouts detained the Velorn envoy without violence. He is in a tent with warm food and cold silence. The Arkenor lieutenant has been moved to a separate location. Elowen has not yet decided what to say to either of them.
Caeris Thorne has written a regency proclamation in his own hand and sealed it. He has told no one. He keeps it beneath the floorboard of his sleeping chamber and has not slept since writing it.
Rowen's diving teams report a structure beneath the eastern approach — stone arches, intact, twenty feet below current waterline, with what appears to be a sealed iron door at their center.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld Dwarves — Deep Entity vs All Surface Factions
active_tensions.description: The gate breathes warmth. Kessa no longer eats. The hammering's cadence is a name being spoken upward through stone. Ironmaul's Druid letters are the last diplomatic rope dangling over an open shaft.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Succession Collapse and the Admiral's Advance
active_tensions.description: Caeris holds an unsigned regency proclamation under his floorboards. The Admiral meets minor lords offshore. Aldric and Roderic are still sealed together. The Seer is two days away and the world is not waiting.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Velorn Defiance and Lythara's Authority
active_tensions.description: The Velorn envoy sits in Elowen's tent. Lythara summons the clan heads. If she cannot reassert discipline, the Shadow Court's three-clan unity begins to fracture under the pressure of the Frostvale pivot.

active_tensions.factions: Dreadwind Islands — The Key and the Submerged Lock
active_tensions.description: Twenty feet below the sinking island's waterline: stone arches and a sealed iron door. Rowen has the key. The island is still descending. Whatever is behind that door may not remain accessible much longer.

character_updates.name: Seer
character_updates.status: On the mountain road, walking north toward the Twin Cities. No escort. Carries only staff and message. Will not arrive for approximately 2 ticks. Knowledge of world events lags from departure point.

character_updates.name: Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Has written a sealed regency proclamation held under a floorboard. Has not slept. Still stands his vigil at the chamber door by day. The Gnome shadow-agent is now following the Seer toward him without his knowledge.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Has stopped eating. Her neck symbols have begun appearing on her palms. She speaks less each day. Ironmaul watches her between letters and does not know which crisis to solve first.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Second missive dispatched to Druids with the hammering transcribed in musical notation. Has not slept two days running. The garrison has lost three more soldiers to desertion at dawn.

character_updates.name: Sovereign Elowen Silverleaf
character_updates.status: Holds the Velorn envoy in a tent on the ridge. Has sent for a Druid emissary specializing in deep-earth resonance. Has not yet replied to the Shadow Court about anything.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: His divers have found the submerged stone arches and iron door. He sits on deck at night with the key in his hand. He has not opened his chest again. He is counting how many more days before the door goes too deep.

faction_morale.reason: Three more desertions. Kessa no longer eats. Ironmaul's sleepless correspondence is the only active response to a crisis too large for any one person. The garrison sees no end.

Lythara's emergency clan summons signals that Velorn's defiance is already known internally. The clan heads know she knows. The question is whether she can reassert authority without fracturing alliances.
Elowen's decisive detention of the Velorn envoy and the requested Druid consultation signal competence. Soldiers on the ridge remain steady though quietly anxious about Lostfeld's direction.
Caeris's secret regency proclamation is both a preparation and a confession of desperation. Court factionalism deepens as Tidefall's offshore meeting with minor lords nears.
The offshore meeting invitation to minor lords has energized the Admiral's inner circle. The harbor-treaty draft is nearly complete. Dur Khadur's mercenary offer adds teeth to the transition plan.
The emergency inner Conclave session signals growing urgency — and growing comprehension. The Grand Druid is close to something, though he cannot name it yet. The tension is electric.
The submerged door discovery has galvanized Rowen's captains. They now believe the king has found something that could change the balance of sea power. Discipline is strong, purpose is sharp.
The shadow-agent tailing the Seer is a perfect intelligence opportunity. Whatever the Seer carries to Caeris, the Syndicate will know it first — or simultaneously. Leverage accumulates.

faction_resources.pressure: Internal clan tension consuming influence. Velorn's unauthorized action costs political capital.
Druid emissary request costs gold and influence. Ridge maintenance ongoing upkeep drain.
Court paralysis erodes economic confidence. Minor lords defecting to Tidefall correspondence leaks influence fast.
Offshore meeting costs gold for neutral vessel and courier. Gains in influence from minor lord recruitment offset it.
Desertions reduce military. Missive correspondence costs gold. Kessa's deterioration is a morale drain with no resource remedy.
Shadow-agent deployment costs gold and military. Intelligence gain expected to exceed cost within 2 ticks.
Emergency Conclave session and emissary preparation drain gold and materials. Rising influence from correspondence activity.
Diving operations cost materials and food. Extended anchorage drains supplies. Rising morale partially offsets resource pressure.
Envoy maintenance aboard Tidefall flagship costs gold. Mercenary offer investment may yield political stake in succession.
Doubled border watch costs military upkeep. Precautionary investment with no immediate return.
Scout band deployment eastward costs food and military. Opportunity-driven — low risk, unclear reward.
Court infiltration investment costs gold. Influence rising as succession chaos deepens. Their strongest operating environment.

trade_routes.exchange: Grain and timber for harbor access and naval escort
trade_routes.effect: Political paralysis slows Twin Cities shipments. Tidefall exploits delays as leverage in draft treaty.

trade_routes.exchange: Glass goods and pass-crossing rights for forest materials
trade_routes.effect: Stable mutual benefit; Gnomes gain Elowen's tacit goodwill, useful while the Seer is in transit.

trade_routes.exchange: Courier services and intelligence for gold
trade_routes.effect: Caeris's halt of Gnome correspondence has reduced this route's volume and the Syndicate's income from it.

trade_routes.exchange: Ore and stonework for surface food and materials
trade_routes.effect: Farrock's doubled border watch slows transit. Lostfeld's output declining as workers desert or refuse deep shifts.

trade_routes.exchange: Raiding tolls and navigation rights for passage near island waters
trade_routes.effect: Extended anchorage near sinking island creates a de facto blockade of eastern approach lanes, affecting Tidefall supply margin.

trade_routes.exchange: Herbal knowledge and ritual services for forest sanctuary and food
trade_routes.effect: Emissary request from Elowen accelerates this route's significance. Mutual interest deepening.

goals: Deliver truth as witnessed
Reach those who need warning before the moment passes
Survive his own knowledge
faction_identities.doctrine: The Seer is a witness, not a prophet. He reports what the world is doing to itself. He moves through it as a man, not a messenger of gods.
faction_identities.personality: Burdened, deliberate, increasingly uncertain whether his warnings help or only mark the wound.

goals: Maintain Queen Lythara's absolute authority over the clans
Expand Faerwood's sphere of influence
Neutralize the Glenhaven threat without open war
faction_identities.doctrine: Power is shadow — it shapes outcomes without declaring itself. The clan that acts in the open has already lost.
faction_identities.personality: Cold, patient, factional, prone to internal rivalry when external pressure is absent.

goals: Contain the Deep Entity
Find allies before isolation becomes extinction
Preserve Kessa — she is the only one who can speak to it
faction_identities.doctrine: Stone endures. But stone can also be broken from below. Endurance without adaptation is just slow collapse.
faction_identities.personality: Stubborn, proud, currently terrified. Ironmaul holds the shape of authority while the substance crumbles.

goals: Protect Frostvale from both Shadow Court pressure and Lostfeld contagion
Secure Druid knowledge about the deep-earth phenomenon
Maintain Elowen's personal authority without revealing strategic pivots prematurely
faction_identities.doctrine: The forest does not explain itself. Neither should those who tend it.
faction_identities.personality: Wise, contained, occasionally dangerously slow to commit.

goals: Preserve the Thorne dynasty against Tidefall's encroachment
Stabilize the succession before Roderic dies or is incapacitated
Caeris must find his authority before it is claimed by others
faction_identities.doctrine: Legitimacy is the only weapon that cannot be forged. It must be inherited or earned in sight of witnesses.
faction_identities.personality: Proud, currently paralyzed, historically powerful — unaccustomed to this kind of slow dissolution.

goals: Formalize political dominance over Twin Cities through the succession crisis
Bind minor lords into a coalition before the throne stabilizes
Use Dur Khadur's mercenary offer to give the transition teeth
faction_identities.doctrine: The sea does not ask permission to rise. The Admiral governs the tide, not the shore.
faction_identities.personality: Ambitious, pragmatic, currently ascendant and aware of it.

goals: Comprehend the pre-Accord book fully before acting on its contents
Understand the connection between the Entity's language and the text
Maintain the Gloomspire alliance as operational cover
faction_identities.doctrine: Nature is neither good nor evil. The mandate we receive from it is neither comfortable nor optional.
faction_identities.personality: Morally ambiguous, intellectually driven, capable of great patience and sudden terrible action.

goals: Maintain the mountain pass monopoly
Convert all significant intelligence into leverage or gold
Stay invisible while remaining indispensable
faction_identities.doctrine: The pass is ours because no one else can cross it. Everything else we build on that fact.
faction_identities.personality: Calculating, patient, socially charming and privately predatory.

goals: Use the key to access whatever lies beneath the sinking island before it descends too far
Convert that discovery into naval or political leverage against Tidefall
Restore Rowen's legitimacy as Pirate King through an act of historic significance
faction_identities.doctrine: The sea gives and the sea takes. A king who can read the sea is worth more than one who can only sail it.
faction_identities.personality: Bold, loyal to Rowen personally, superstitious in productive ways.

goals: Position within whichever faction emerges dominant from the succession crisis
Accumulate personal influence over key noble figures
Remain hidden while shaping the outcome
faction_identities.doctrine: Every throne needs a shadow behind it. We are that shadow, and we are patient beyond mortal reckoning.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.
